title: China-to-Nigeria Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: China GB/T Baseline vs Nigerian Market Entry Package
rows.target_requirement: A Nigerian market-entry package for a solar PV inverter normally combines SONCAP import conformity documents where the product is regulated, SON product certification or registration where applicable, accepted safety and EMC evidence, Nigerian-language or importer documentation where requested, and NERC or utility project acceptance for grid-connected or mini-grid use. Passing one gate does not waive the others.
target_standards: SON product certification / registration where applicable
NERC Grid Code, Distribution Code, or Mini-Grid Regulations 2026 where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: A typical China-origin file set may include GB/T 37408-2019, GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004-2018, IEC 62109 CB reports, CQC/CGC voluntary certificates, Chinese EMC reports, Chinese labels/manuals, and State Grid or China Southern Power Grid project records. This is a useful baseline but not a Nigerian approval package.
rows.gap: The export gap is procedural, documentary, and project-specific. Build a Nigeria crosswalk before shipment: SONCAP regulated-product status, product certificate route, accepted safety and EMC reports, importer details, labels and manuals, NERC grid or mini-grid route, 50 Hz settings, commissioning records, and project-owner acceptance.

mandatory_status.target: Mixed but cumulative — SONCAP is mandatory for regulated imports; SON certification or registration applies where required; NERC / utility approval is mandatory for covered grid-connected or mini-grid projects.
mandatory_status.cn: Mixed — many China baseline inverter documents are voluntary or project-driven and do not transfer automatically to Nigeria.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not treat the China GB/T and CB file set as a Nigerian approval file. Nigeria needs a SONCAP / SON / NERC-specific compliance package matched to the product and project.

rows.requirement: Nigeria 50 Hz Settings and EMC / Power-Quality Evidence
rows.target_requirement: NERC lists the Nigerian Grid Code and Distribution Code as electricity-sector instruments. A solar PV inverter for Nigeria should be configured and commissioned for the Nigerian network profile, including 50 Hz operation, local voltage class, frequency and voltage trip windows, reconnection timing, and any utility power-quality or EMC evidence requested for the project. Where SONCAP, SON, a utility, or a project owner requests EMC evidence, IEC 61000-series or PV-specific IEC 62920 reports may be relevant, but the accepted standard list must be confirmed for the actual model and route.
target_standards: IEC 62920, where required for PV power conversion equipment
rows.china_equivalent: China inverter files often show 50 Hz capability under GB/T 37408, GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004, or factory datasheets, and may include GB/T or GB EMC reports, CNAS laboratory reports, IEC 62920 evidence, or CE EMC evidence. These are useful supporting documents only if the Nigerian review route accepts the standard editions, lab accreditation, model coverage, firmware, and installation configuration.
china_standards: China GB / GB/T EMC test reports
rows.gap: Do not treat generic 50 Hz capability or a China / CE EMC report as Nigerian project acceptance. Document the country profile, site voltage, frequency windows, anti-islanding timing, reconnection delay, power-factor mode, firmware version, conducted and radiated emissions, immunity, harmonics or flicker where applicable, and commissioning test results.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where required by the NERC code, distribution company, connection agreement, SONCAP, SON, utility, or project owner; not confirmed as a standalone whole-unit Nigerian EMC mark for every inverter.
mandatory_status.cn: Partial only — China 50 Hz and EMC evidence can support compatibility review but does not prove Nigerian project settings or acceptance.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A China 50 Hz datasheet or EMC file is only a starting point. Nigerian deployment needs the actual local grid profile, accepted EMC / power-quality evidence where required, and commissioning evidence accepted by the network operator or approval route.

rows.requirement: NERC Grid Code and Distribution Connection Review
rows.target_requirement: NERC lists the Nigerian Grid Code and Distribution Code as regulatory instruments, with the Grid Code published on the NERC codes page and a Draft Grid Code Version 3 posted in 2025. A PV inverter used in a grid-connected project should be checked against the current grid or distribution connection instrument, utility interconnection conditions, protection settings, power-quality limits, and commissioning evidence required by the relevant network operator.
target_standards: Draft Grid Code Version 3 and NESIS Regulations Version 2, where adopted or requested
rows.china_equivalent: China grid evidence commonly references GB/T 37408, GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004, and State Grid or China Southern Power Grid project settings. These files support technical review but do not authorize a Nigerian grid connection.
china_standards: State Grid / China Southern Power Grid connection procedures
rows.gap: The main gap is local project acceptance: anti-islanding, under/over-voltage and frequency windows, ride-through behavior, reactive power, harmonics, protection coordination, settings lockout, and commissioning records must match the Nigerian network operator's accepted grid-code file.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected projects to the extent required by NERC instruments, the distribution or transmission licensee, and the project connection agreement.
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent — China grid-code evidence does not create Nigerian utility permission to connect.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat grid-code review as a project gate separate from SONCAP import clearance and IEC 62109 safety. China grid reports must be mapped to the Nigerian operator's accepted settings before energization.

rows.requirement: NERC Mini-Grid Regulations 2026 for Solar Inverter Projects
rows.target_requirement: NERC lists Mini Grid Regulations 2026 and explains that isolated mini-grids may be up to 5 MW per site, interconnected mini-grids may be up to 10 MW per site, projects not more than 100 kW use a registration route, and larger systems require a permit. Solar mini-grid projects also need environmental and safety compliance evidence under the NERC mini-grid framework.
target_standards: NERC mini-grid registration route for projects not more than 100 kW
NERC mini-grid permit route for larger systems
Environmental and safety compliance under the mini-grid framework
rows.china_equivalent: China factory inverter reports, GB/T grid reports, and IEC 62109 safety certificates can support technical due diligence for a Nigerian mini-grid, but they do not replace NERC registration, permits, tariff-model requirements, community or distribution-company agreements, or project environmental and safety evidence.
rows.gap: The gap is project regulatory approval rather than only inverter type testing. Confirm whether the installation is isolated or interconnected, capacity band, registration or permit route, tariff model, interconnection studies, protection settings, ESMP or ESIA needs, and customer-service documentation before equipment procurement.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for mini-grid projects within the NERC mini-grid framework; exact registration or permit route depends on project type and capacity.
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent — China product and grid reports do not replace Nigerian mini-grid registration, permit, tariff, interconnection, or environmental requirements.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Mini-grid use can add NERC project obligations beyond inverter import conformity. Confirm registration or permit status before treating a certified inverter as deployable.

rows.requirement: IEC 62109 Safety Evidence for SONCAP / Project Review
rows.target_requirement: SON product certification is a third-party validation process for conformity with national standards or other requirements. For imported regulated products, SONCAP compliance can use NIS, IEC, ISO, or approved standards. For solar PV inverters, IEC 62109-1:2010, edition 1.0, published 2010-04-28, and IEC 62109-2:2011, edition 1.0, published 2011-06-23, are the internationally used safety standards for PV power converters and inverter particulars, but exporters should confirm whether SON, the IAF, or the project owner requires a specific NIS adoption, IEC CB report, or additional local evidence.
target_standards: SON product certification under SON Act No. 14 of 2015
SONCAP acceptance of applicable NIS, IEC, ISO, or approved standards
rows.china_equivalent: Many China-origin inverter manufacturers already hold IEC 62109 CB reports, CQC or CGC voluntary certificates, and GB/T inverter test reports. These can reduce evidence gaps if accepted by the SONCAP IAF, but they are not a Nigerian product certification or import clearance by themselves.
rows.gap: The gap is acceptance and edition control. Build a safety crosswalk to IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2, verify the exact test-report scope, ratings, environmental limits, transformer or transformerless topology, protective functions, labels, and Nigerian importer details required by the IAF or SON.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where SONCAP or SON product certification applies to the imported inverter; IEC 62109 evidence is an accepted technical route only if the relevant SON / IAF / project process accepts it.
mandatory_status.cn: Mixed — IEC CB and China voluntary certificates may be useful supporting evidence, while China GB/T reports are not Nigerian certification.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC 62109 evidence is important for Nigerian inverter files, but it should be presented through the applicable SONCAP or SON product-certification route rather than treated as automatic market access.

rows.requirement: SONCAP Import Conformity Route for Regulated Solar PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: SONCAP is Nigeria's pre-shipment verification of conformity process for products imported into Nigeria that are subject to SONCAP. SON states that regulated goods require a Product Certificate route, Certificate of Conformity, and SONCAP Certificate for importation and Customs clearance, with testing and verification against applicable NIS, IEC, ISO, or other approved standards.
target_standards: Applicable NIS, IEC, ISO, or approved equivalent standards
rows.china_equivalent: China-origin inverter files often include GB/T 37408, GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004, IEC 62109-1:2010 / IEC 62109-2:2011 CB reports, CQC or CGC voluntary certificates, factory audit records, and Chinese labels. These materials may support a SONCAP submission but do not replace the SONCAP certificate documents required for regulated imports.
rows.gap: The practical gap is import-document control: confirm that the inverter model is a SONCAP-regulated product, choose the correct Route A, B, or C process through an Independent Accredited Firm, prepare model-level test evidence, and obtain the SONCAP Certificate before Nigerian Customs clearance.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for goods that are subject to SONCAP; the SONCAP Certificate is mandatory for Nigerian Customs clearance of SONCAP-regulated products.
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent — China GB/T, CQC, CGC, or CB evidence may support testing review but does not replace SONCAP import documents.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat SONCAP as a shipment and Customs gate for regulated inverter imports. Do not rely on a China technical file alone unless SON or the appointed IAF confirms the applicable route and accepts the evidence.
